meta_title: Impact Windows in Inverrary, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Inverrary, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote today!
h1: Impact Windows for Inverrary Homes: Strength, Style, and Storm-Ready Protection
intro:
Inverrary may sit about seven miles from the Atlantic coastline, but don't let that distance fool you into thinking hurricane season is someone else's problem. When a major storm tracks across South Florida, inland communities like Inverrary face sustained winds, flying debris, and pressure changes that are just as dangerous as anything felt along the shoreline. With a wind zone rating of 170 mph and a storm history that includes the wrath of Hurricane Andrew, Hurricane Wilma, and Hurricane Irma, Inverrary homeowners have every reason to take their window protection seriously. Impact windows aren't a luxury here — they're a practical necessity.

The neighborhoods of Inverrary Country Club and Inverrary Village are home to a mix of well-established single-family residences and condominiums that reflect the community's enduring appeal in northwest Broward County. With average home values hovering around $280,000, your property represents a significant investment — one that deserves the kind of long-term protection that only impact-resistant windows can reliably provide. Roughly half of the homes in this area were built before 1994, which means they predate the sweeping Florida Building Code reforms that followed the catastrophic damage from Hurricane Andrew. Many of those older homes still have standard single-pane or aluminum-frame windows that offer little to no resistance against hurricane-force winds or the debris they carry.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners make the transition from vulnerable windows to certified, hurricane-rated impact products — and we do it with a level of craftsmanship and customer care that has earned us an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau. Whether you live along the fairways of the Inverrary Country Club or in a quiet cul-de-sac in Inverrary Village, our team is ready to assess your home, explain your options, and deliver an installation that meets both Florida Building Code requirements and your personal expectations. This is what it means to work with the Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Inverrary Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing a contractor to install impact windows in your home isn't just about finding the lowest bid — it's about finding a team that shows up prepared, communicates clearly, and stands behind their work long after the last screw is set. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built on exactly that philosophy. With more than 60 years of combined installation experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our crews have encountered virtually every window configuration, wall construction type, and permit challenge that South Florida homes can present. We bring that depth of knowledge to every project we take on in Inverrary.

One of the biggest differentiators between Premier Impact Windows & Roofing and many of our competitors is our commitment to 100% in-house installation. We do not subcontract your project to third-party crews who may lack training, accountability, or proper licensing. Every technician who enters your Inverrary home is a direct employee of Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — vetted, trained, and operating under the supervision of our experienced project managers. This means consistent quality, predictable timelines, and a single point of contact if you ever have a question or concern. With over 500 homes successfully completed across South Florida, our track record speaks for itself.

We also understand that every homeowner in Inverrary has unique needs and a unique budget. That's why we've built a flexible consultation process that starts with a thorough on-site evaluation. We measure every opening, assess your existing frame conditions, discuss your priorities — whether that's maximum energy efficiency, enhanced noise reduction, a specific aesthetic, or all of the above — and then recommend the products and configurations that make the most sense for your specific home. There's no one-size-fits-all approach here, because no two homes on the Inverrary Country Club grounds or in Inverrary Village are exactly alike.

Finally, our licensing and insurance credentials ensure that every installation we perform is fully compliant with Broward County requirements. We handle the permit applications, coordinate inspections with the Broward County Building Division, and keep you informed every step of the way. When the job is done, you'll have documentation of a properly permitted, code-compliant installation — which matters enormously if you ever plan to sell your home or file a wind mitigation insurance claim.

section_history.h2: Inverrary's Storm History Makes a Strong Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Since 1990, Inverrary and the broader northwest Broward County region have experienced no fewer than nine named storms significant enough to warrant attention. The most formative of these was Hurricane Andrew in 1992, a Category 5 storm that reshaped how the entire state of Florida thinks about building codes and residential construction standards. While Andrew's worst damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade, its effects reached well into Broward County, exposing the fragility of pre-code construction and prompting the sweeping regulatory overhaul that followed. Homes in Inverrary that survived Andrew often did so with broken windows, water intrusion, and structural damage that took months — and in some cases years — to fully remediate.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a different kind of lesson. A fast-moving, powerful storm that crossed the Florida peninsula from the Gulf side, Wilma produced sustained winds and gusts that battered Broward County communities with very little warning. Inverrary residents who hadn't upgraded their windows experienced shattered glass, damaged frames, and the kind of interior flooding and wind-driven rain that causes far more long-term damage than the initial storm itself. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked up the spine of the state and brought tropical storm and hurricane-force conditions to virtually every corner of South Florida, including Inverrary. Irma served as a reminder that no inland community is truly insulated from the threat.

The cumulative lesson from Andrew, Wilma, Irma, and the other storms that have passed through this region over the past three decades is clear: homes without impact-rated windows are vulnerable, and that vulnerability carries real financial and safety consequences. Impact windows are specifically engineered to withstand the pressure differentials, wind loads, and debris impact events that these storms generate — keeping your family safe and your home's interior protected even in the most severe conditions.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Inverrary and Broward County
section_code.body:
Inverrary falls within Broward County's jurisdiction and is subject to the Florida Building Code, which mandates hurricane-resistant protection for all exterior openings in residential structures. While this area is not classified as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation primarily applied to Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closer to the coast — that does not mean standard windows are acceptable. Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County still demand that all windows and doors meet rigorous large missile impact testing, pressure rating standards, and wind load specifications appropriate for a 170 mph wind zone. Products must carry valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) documentation or be listed in the Florida Product Approval database.

For homeowners in Inverrary, this means that any window replacement or new installation requires a building permit issued through the Broward County Building Division. The permitting process involves submitting product specifications, installation drawings, and contractor credentials before work begins. After installation, a county inspector must visit the property to verify that the work was completed in accordance with approved plans and applicable code sections. Skipping this step — which some unlicensed contractors encourage in order to reduce costs and turnaround time — can result in fines, required removal and reinstallation, and serious complications when selling the home or filing an insurance claim.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on behalf of our Inverrary clients. We are fully familiar with Broward County Building Division procedures, documentation requirements, and inspection scheduling. Because roughly half of the homes in Inverrary predate the 1994 code reforms, special attention is sometimes required when upgrading openings in older structural configurations — our experienced team knows how to navigate these situations efficiently and correctly, ensuring your installation is fully compliant from start to finish.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premier Products Built for Inverrary's Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted impact window brands in the South Florida market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines are engineered specifically for the demanding conditions found in hurricane-prone regions like Broward County, and both carry the certifications necessary to satisfy Florida Building Code requirements for Inverrary's 170 mph wind zone. When you work with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not choosing between bargain-bin products and premium options — you're choosing between two high-quality brands, each with its own strengths and aesthetic profiles.

ESW impact windows are built with multi-chamber frame systems that provide exceptional structural rigidity while also delivering outstanding thermal performance. The laminated glass used in ESW products bonds two panes of glass around a durable interlayer — typically PVB or SGP — that holds the glass together upon impact, preventing shattering and maintaining the window's integrity even when struck by wind-driven debris. Low-E coatings on the glass surface reflect infrared heat and block UV radiation, helping to keep Inverrary homes cooler in the summer months and reducing the workload on air conditioning systems. For a community like Inverrary, where homes are occupied year-round and energy costs are a consistent concern, these thermal benefits are just as important as the hurricane protection.

CWI impact windows offer complementary performance with a focus on design flexibility and a wide range of frame colors and configurations. Whether you're looking for single-hung, double-hung, casement, awning, or picture windows, CWI's product lineup covers virtually every opening type found in Inverrary's varied residential architecture. Both ESW and CWI windows have been tested to meet or exceed the impact resistance, pressure rating, and water infiltration standards required for Broward County installations. Our team will help you determine which brand and configuration best matches your home's specific requirements, your aesthetic preferences, and your long-term performance goals.

section_cost.h2: What Inverrary Homeowners Can Expect to Pay for Impact Windows — and How to Finance Them
section_cost.body:
The cost of installing impact windows in an Inverrary home depends on several variables: the number and size of openings, the product line and frame finish selected, existing frame conditions, and any structural modifications required during installation. For a typical Inverrary home with an average value around $280,000, a full-home impact window installation generally ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 or more, depending on the scope of the project. Homes in Inverrary Country Club or Inverrary Village that feature larger square footage, custom window shapes, or architectural detailing may fall toward the higher end of that range. A detailed on-site estimate from our team will provide you with a precise figure based on your specific home.

For homeowners concerned about the upfront investment, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options designed to make impact windows accessible regardless of your current budget situation. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified applicants. This means you can begin protecting your home — and immediately start benefiting from lower energy bills and reduced insurance premiums — without waiting years to accumulate the full cost in savings. When you factor in the ongoing monthly savings on utilities and insurance, many Inverrary homeowners find that their impact windows effectively pay for themselves over a five-to-ten-year period.

From a pure return-on-investment perspective, impact windows are one of the most financially sound upgrades an Inverrary homeowner can make. Real estate professionals consistently report that impact windows are a desirable feature in Broward County's resale market, often contributing directly to faster sales and stronger offers. Combined with insurance savings that can run into hundreds of dollars per year and energy savings that reduce monthly utility costs, the financial case for impact windows in Inverrary is compelling — both in the short term and over the life of your home.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Windows Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance in Inverrary
section_insurance.body:
One of the most tangible financial benefits of installing impact windows in Inverrary is the effect they can have on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer discounts to policyholders who have made documented improvements to their home's wind resistance — and impact windows are one of the most highly valued upgrades in the eyes of insurers. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and permitted, you can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector. The resulting wind mitigation report documents your new windows and the level of protection they provide, and that report is then submitted to your insurance carrier to trigger applicable discounts.

In Broward County, wind mitigation credits for impact-rated windows and doors can produce meaningful annual savings — in many cases ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars per year depending on your carrier, coverage level, and the scope of the opening protection. Major carriers operating in Inverrary's market, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, all have wind mitigation discount programs that recognize impact window installations. For older Inverrary homes — particularly the approximately 50% of local housing stock built before 1994 — these savings can be especially significant, since those properties often carry higher base premiums due to their age and original construction standards.

It's also worth noting that impact windows can help you avoid the surcharges and coverage complications that increasingly come with standard windows in South Florida's challenging insurance market. As carriers continue to exit or restrict coverage in Florida, homes with documented hurricane protection features are viewed as lower-risk properties — a distinction that can affect not just your premium but your ability to secure favorable coverage in the first place. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary documentation and product information to support your wind mitigation inspection and insurance discount process.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows if I live in Inverrary, which is inland from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Inverrary sits in a 170 mph wind zone and has been affected by multiple major hurricanes including Andrew, Wilma, and Irma. Distance from the coast does not reduce the risk from wind-driven debris, pressure changes, or sustained hurricane-force winds. Florida Building Code requires hurricane-rated window protection for all Broward County homes, regardless of coastal proximity. Impact windows provide essential protection for both inland and coastal properties.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Inverrary home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For an average Inverrary home, a full-home impact window installation typically ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the number of openings, window sizes, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified applicants. We recommend scheduling a free on-site estimate to get an accurate, detailed quote tailored to your specific home and needs.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact windows in Inverrary, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes, a building permit is required for all impact window installations in Inverrary under Broward County Building Division rules. Permits ensure the work meets Florida Building Code standards for your 170 mph wind zone. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from submitting the application and product documentation to coordinating the required post-installation inspection. You don't have to navigate the bureaucracy alone.

faqs.q: How long does it take to install impact windows in my Inverrary home?
faqs.a: The timeline varies depending on the size of your home and the number of windows being replaced. For a typical Inverrary single-family home, the actual installation is usually completed in one to three days. Permit processing through Broward County typically adds a few weeks to the overall project timeline before installation can begin. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed throughout the process and works efficiently to minimize any disruption to your daily routine.

faqs.q: Will installing impact windows really lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Inverrary?
faqs.a: Yes — Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts to homeowners who install hurricane-rated impact windows. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and permitted, you can obtain a wind mitigation inspection report and submit it to your carrier. Broward County homeowners commonly see annual savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars depending on their insurer and coverage level. These savings, combined with energy efficiency benefits, contribute to a strong long-term return on your investment.
